fil: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Filipino.
film: noun. A thin layer of some substance; a pellicle; a membranous covering, causing opacity.
filmy: adjective. Resembling or made of a thin film; gauzy.
fils: adjective. Used after a proper name that is common to a father and his son to indicate that the son is being referred to rather than the
flimsy: adjective. Likely to bend or break under pressure; easily damaged; frail, unsubstantial.
flip: noun. A maneuver which rotates an object end over end.
imp: verb. To engraft or plant (a plant or part of one, a sapling, etc.).
impi: noun. A group of Zulu (or other Bantu) warriors; a detachment of armed men.
imply: verb. To have as a necessary consequence; to lead to (something) as a consequence.
ism: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Masimasi.
limp: verb. To walk lamely, as if favoring one leg.
mis: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for unencoded languages.
pis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pijin.
psi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeast Pashayi.
sip: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sikkimese.
slimy: adjective. Of or pertaining to slime.
slip: verb. To lose one’s traction on a slippery surface; to slide due to a lack of friction.
spy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sabaot.
syli: noun. The currency of Guinea between 1971 and 1985, subdivided into 100 cauris.
yip: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pholo.
yips: noun. A nervous condition which prevents a sportsperson from playing properly; especially a condition which causes a golfer to miss an easy
